perturbation.original_text: Recipient expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Examinee expressly agrees not to use the Secret Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Information' creates confusion. The document defines 'Confidential Information', but never defines 'Secret Information'. It's unclear if these terms are interchangeable, thus creating ambiguity about what the examinee is agreeing not to use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Examinee expressly agrees not to use the Secret Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Confidential Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Informati on is granted hereby. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. Recipient agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. Recipient hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information', which is defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces this with 'Secret Information', which is not defined anywhere in the document, as explained in the 'Perturbation Explanation'. The 'Contradicted Text' states requirements regarding 'Confidential Information'. By changing the term, it becomes unclear whether those requirements apply to 'Secret Information' as well, creating a contradiction and ambiguity. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract effectively uses two terms that might be intended to mean the same thing, without explicitly defining them as such, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Informati on is granted hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: All Secret Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Secret Information' in the first instance creates a contradiction within the same sentence. The first half refers to 'Secret Information' as belonging to the Company, but the second half still refers to 'Confidential Information' for which no rights are granted. Since 'Secret Information' is undefined, this is a clear contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Examinee expressly agrees not to use the Secret Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Secret Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. Recipient agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. Recipient hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Information' in the changed text creates a contradiction because the contradicted text heavily relies on the term 'Confidential Information' being well-defined and used consistently. The changed text introduces 'Secret Information', which is undefined, then immediately refers to 'Confidential Information' in the same sentence, creating ambiguity and undermining the established definition and usage of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted section. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The test-taker agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Recipient' to 'test-taker' introduces a degree of ambiguity. It becomes unclear whether 'Recipient' in the earlier parts of the agreement and 'test-taker' are meant to refer to the same entity, leading to potential confusion about which obligations and rights apply to each.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Examinee expressly agrees not to use the Secret Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Secret Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. The test-taker agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. Recipient hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Recipient' to 'test-taker' creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text repeatedly refers to 'Recipient', outlining specific obligations and rights regarding Confidential Information. By changing the term to 'test-taker' in the changed text, it creates ambiguity about which entity those obligations apply to. The contradicted text mentions 'Recipient' multiple times, including agreeing to maintain confidentiality, prevent unauthorized dissemination, comply with export controls, and acknowledging potential harm from unauthorized disclosure. Because the Contradicted Text also includes the Changed Text, the agreement is internally inconsistent, creating a strong degree of ambiguity that would likely result in unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain.
perturbation.changed_text: The Applicant hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Recipient' with 'Applicant' further dilutes the cohesion of the agreement. By using 'Applicant' it is unclear whether the agreement's conditions and repercussions apply to someone before, during, or after the exam. As the conditions applied for what a recipient must accept and follow during an exam, this replacement leads to in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Examinee expressly agrees not to use the Secret Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Secret Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. The test-taker agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. The Applicant hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Recipient,' aligning with the contradicted text which repeatedly refers to 'Recipient' and outlines their obligations. The changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'Applicant.' While the contradicted text initially defines obligations for the 'Recipient', the changed text introduces 'Applicant' which is not consistently defined or used throughout the contradicted section. This creates a contradiction because it's now unclear whether the obligations defined for 'Recipient' in the contradicted section also apply to the 'Applicant', leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, The client agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'The client' creates uncertainty regarding which entity is subject to the injunctive relief. If 'The client' is distinct from 'Recipient,' 'Examinee,' 'Test-taker' and 'Applicant' mentioned earlier, it is unsure if the injunction applies to them, and which 'client' is being referred to, and this directly contradicts the document's intention of obligating only the test takers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Examinee expressly agrees not to use the Secret Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Secret Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. The test-taker agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. The Applicant hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, The client agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'Recipient' agrees to injunctive relief. The changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'The client'. The contradicted text contains 'Recipient,' 'Examinee,' 'Test-taker' and 'Applicant'. Replacing 'Recipient' with 'The client' introduces ambiguity, as it's unclear whether 'The client' refers to any of the other specified entities or a new, undefined entity. This creates a conflict because it is now uncertain to whom the injunctive relief applies, therefore there is a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.'
